# Live Music Coder — LLM Context

## What is this?
A browser-based live coding music IDE. Users write code to make music in real time. Features a CodeMirror 6 editor, React Flow node graph, 4 audio engines, Canvas 2D visualizers, and audio-reactive creatures called Beatlings.

## Live URL
https://live-music-coder.pro

## Tech Stack
React 19, TypeScript 5.9, Vite 8, Tailwind CSS 4, Zustand 5, CodeMirror 6, React Flow, @strudel/core, Tone.js, Web Audio API, WebMidi.js, Canvas 2D, i18next (DE/EN/ES), idb, lz-string, @octokit/rest, Framer Motion 12, Vitest.

## Architecture
- Code editor is source of truth; node graph is derived view
- Engine orchestrator with shared AudioContext routes to 4 engine adapters
- Atomic Design: atoms → molecules → organisms → layouts → pages
- Design tokens via CSS custom properties (Canvas uses VIZ_COLORS literals)
- Beatling creatures have neural network + Conway's Game of Life dual-brain
- Brain code adapted from wm-lifegame project (copied, not imported)

## Audio Engines
- Strudel: pattern-based live coding (@strudel/core + @strudel/webaudio)
- Tone.js: high-level synths and effects
- Web Audio: raw browser API (oscillators, filters, gain nodes)
- MIDI: external device output only (WebMidi.js)

## Beatling Species (6)
Beatling (drums/bounce), Looplet (loops/orbit), Synthling (melody/flow), Glitchbit (noise/glitch), Wavelet (bass/wave), Codefly (typing/swarm). Each spawns based on audio features and evolves through 5 stages: Egg → Baby → Adult → Elder → Ascended.

## Persistence
- IndexedDB autosave (idb)
- URL sharing via lz-string compression (#code=...)
- GitHub Gist save/load (@octokit/rest) with PAT in sessionStorage/localStorage
- Audio recording via MediaRecorder → WebM

## Key Constraints
- Dark mode only (music production aesthetic)
- NEVER hardcode colors/spacing — use design tokens
- This is Vite + React SPA, NOT Next.js
- AGPL-3.0 license (Strudel dependency)
- 122 source files (TS/TSX), 104 tests across 19 test files
- Colocated tests (foo.test.ts next to foo.ts)

## Routes (8)
- `/` → Landing page (hero, features, examples)
- `/editor` → Main IDE (code editor, node graph, visualizers, Beatlings)
- `/editor#code=...` → Load shared project from URL hash
- `/docs` → Documentation hub
- `/docs/:sectionId` → Individual doc section
- `/samples` → Sample library browser
- `/examples` → Curated code examples per engine
- `/legal` → Impressum, Datenschutz, Lizenzen
